creator: email@luiz.eng.br
text:
I investigated this a little more today. I dug a little further on the kernel code and I think I narrowed the issue down to the finalize_nhop() function in rt.c.

Comparing the rtsock code with the new netlink code, I saw that rt_getifa_family() looks very similar to the finalize_nhop() function, with one big difference: the rtsock code calls ifaof_ifpforaddr() and then in_findlocal() while the netlink code calls ifaof_ifpforaddr() twice.

Checking further on the nexthop information on both systems, I noticed that there is actually a difference. 
On FreeBSD 13, the source IP is defined as a private IPv4 address, chosen from one of the VLAN interfaces (IPv4 traffic originating on the host is only working because I have a pf nat rule that SNATed this private range to the loopback address. Without the rule, the source IP is actually the private one).
```
[luiz@lb-ham-1 ~]$ netstat -noW6
Nexthop data

Internet6:
Idx   Type         IFA                           Gateway                        Flags      Use Mtu       Netif   Addrif Refcnt Prepend
...
23           v6/gw 10.42.80.35                   fe80::c8fe:25ff:feca:0%vtnet0 G1        96249   1500   vtnet0             2
```


On FreeBSD 14, the source IP is the link-local IPv6 address:
```
[luiz@lb-ham-2 ~]$ netstat -noW6
Nexthop data

Internet6:
Idx   Type         IFA                           Gateway                        Flags      Use Mtu       Netif   Addrif Refcnt Prepend
...
15           v6/gw fe80::c8fe:1ff:fe8f:100%vtnet0 fe80::c8fe:25ff:feca:0%vtnet0 G          121   1500   vtnet0             2
```

Do you see a way to fix this issue? I am not very familiar with this part of the kernel code, but I think if you point me in the right direction, I could try to implement a fix for it.
